description: Pan Desktop post-install overlays applied to upstream Hermes Agent files that have Unix-only assumptions. Each overlay is applied only when the target file's SHA256 matches the 'upstreamSha256' below (pristine upstream). Drift is detected and skipped with a warning. See M1.1-#008 (fcntl) and M1.1-#009 (AF_UNIX / /tmp paths) for rationale.

overlays.reason: fcntl -> msvcrt for Windows file locking (mirrors cron/scheduler.py:19-27 pattern already in upstream).
Helper module for AF_UNIX<->AF_PIPE cross-platform IPC (new file, not in upstream).

AF_UNIX -> multiprocessing.connection (AF_PIPE on Windows) with per-invocation authkey propagated via env, not argv. Pops HERMES_IPC_* from os.environ after Client connect so grandchild subprocesses cannot inherit the credentials. Also flips SANDBOX_AVAILABLE so the code-execution sandbox works on Windows.

Add PID suffix to the POSIX /tmp upload buffer path to avoid concurrent-upload collisions. Note: the path stays /tmp literally because it is a POSIX path interpreted by the REMOTE sandbox's bash via self.terminal(), not a host filesystem path. Pan Desktop runs on a Windows host, but the sandbox is always Linux.

Adds Windows-native dangerous-command coverage: Level 2 CATASTROPHIC_PATTERNS (28 entries) and 31 new Level 1 DANGEROUS_PATTERNS entries. Adds detect_catastrophic_command, check_command_approval (ApprovalCheck dataclass with level 0/1/2).
Pan Desktop approval event helper — SSE events + registry for Level 1/2 approvals.
Pan Desktop approval HTTP routes — POST/GET /v1/approvals/{id} and GET /v1/approvals.
